text: The surgeon mobilizes the sigmoid and descending colon up to the splenic flexure, then begins dissection at the lower sigmoid and rectosigmoid junction, maintaining the plane along embryological lines into the mesorectum. The retrorectal mass is visible laterally, prompting a wider lateral dissection. The mass is found to be free from the sacrum, allowing for adequate posterior margins. The ureter is identified and preserved during dissection up to the pelvic floor. The surgeon completes the anterior and lateral dissections, connecting the lines of dissection.
